element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Fundamental movement established before dashing mechanic
Enables dash-jumping; players without dash in early MMX1 portion use slope-jumping as substitute
Vertical traversal; double triangle-hop technique unlocks advanced movement
Simple projectile patterns; introduces hit detection and evasion
Boss-specific attack patterns; weapon weakness system; defeating boss grants new weapon
Dynamic weapons system; player toggles between X-Buster and acquired weapons

element_introduction_order.element: Charged weapons (charged X-Buster, charged special weapons)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Increases damage output; enables weapon glitches (passing through enemies with charged Speed Burner, etc.)
Grants special abilities (dash enhancement, armor plating); collectible power-ups
Water hopping technique (tap jump repeatedly) crosses water without diving; alternative to submersion
Jump while walking (not dashing) down slope for height boost; provides horizontal momentum boost
Shorter invincibility windows; weapon choice affects defeat speed

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Post-game after defeating 8 initial maverick bosses
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Complex multi-phase boss fight; requires mastery of all acquired weapons and mechanics
Easter egg move accessible only to skilled players; high damage output

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching safe platform after enemy gauntlet
Completing stage and returning to stage select
typical_tension_triggers: Boss music begins; final stage gate before boss encounter
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Non-linear stage order (8 stages selectable in any order) allows player agency in tension management; each stage follows traditional Mega Man architecture of escalating hazards toward boss; Sigma rematch represents final tension peak

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Armor upgrades and sub-tanks scattered off main path; wall-scaling and dash movement enable secret areas; Dr. Light capsule in Wheel Gator's stage opens at variable timing (2-second window), rewarding patience and positional awareness
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Overhangs and narrow ceiling gaps signal air-dash passages; U-shaped ceiling sections enable double air-dash technique to reach hidden areas; sloped terrain indicates alternate route or early-access passage

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of dash, wall-scaling, and basic enemy patterns; Flame Mammoth's stage offers alternate path reducing immediate difficulty; players can select easiest boss first
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Eight maverick stages of increasing complexity; enemy patterns accelerate; boss patterns demand weapon-switching strategy; armor upgrades become critical for damage mitigation
difficulty_curve.late_game: Sigma fortress stages with miniboss rematches; Sigma multi-phase boss requiring mastery of all weapons; final rematch demands execution of advanced techniques (lucky RNG manipulation against bosses, charged weapon switching)
notable_spikes: First Sigma encounter (introduces multi-phase boss pattern)
Sigma's Broken Sword Defense phase (requires specific weapon strategy)
Miniboss rematches in Sigma's fortress (compressed damage windows due to long invincibility post-hit)

notable_level_patterns.description: Open-ended selection (any order); each stage themed around maverick boss; escalating hazard density; boss at stage conclusion grants weapon keyed to next stage's weakness; slopes, water, and terrain hazards teach movement mechanics
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Flame Mammoth's stage (alternate path shortcut); Bubble Crab's stage (water mechanics + Speed Burner weapon path)

notable_level_patterns.description: Linear progression; miniboss rematches with extended invincibility windows; terrain combinations require all learned techniques; final Sigma encounter in multiple phases

Spike shafts, moving platforms, water sections arranged to block direct paths; wall-scaling and air-dash provide vertical bypass; dashing uncharged X-Buster technique enables shield-like protection while traversing hazards
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Spike shaft sections (no-damage hit technique available)

notable_level_patterns.description: Aquatic terrain traversable by diving or water-hopping (tap jump repeatedly); Speed Burner charge enables faster water crossing; fish enemy lasers bypass collision detection when high-speed dash active
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Bubble Crab's stage (giant fish laser passage)

sources_used: tasvideos (GameResources/SNES/MegaManX - advanced mechanics and level design tricks)
wikipedia (Mega Man X video game - gameplay mechanics, boss structure, stage order flexibility)
